channel.title: In-Context Learning on Chen Kai Blog
channel.description: Recent content in In-Context Learning on Chen Kai Blog

item.title: NLP (7): Prompt Engineering and In-Context Learning
item.description: The same model can produce a sharp answer or a confident hallucination. The difference is rarely the weights – it is the framing. A vague request like “analyze this text” gets you a generic summary; a prompt with a role, two clean examples, and a strict output schema gets you something a parser can consume. Prompt engineering is the discipline of turning that gap into a repeatable system instead of a lucky shot.
